params.description: Horizontal picture graph appears. Completed Warmup graph showing 3 categories with even values. Key visible: Each star = 2. Apples=4 symbols, Bananas=3 symbols, Oranges=5 symbols.
Data table appears alongside graph showing same data in numeric format.

steps.text: You already read this graph. You counted symbols. Each symbol shows 2 items. This is called a SCALED picture graph.
But HOW do you know each symbol shows 2? Let's find out.

elements.description: Horizontal scaled picture graph in reading mode. 3 categories with even values. Key shows scale of 2.
Data table showing same data in numeric format.

steps.text: Every graph has a KEY. The key is like a decoder. It tells you what each symbol MEANS.
This key says: each star equals 2. That 2 is called the SCALE.
Scale of 1 means each symbol represents 1 item. So what does scale of 2 mean?
This graph has a scale of 2. What does that mean?

validator.description: Student selected correct definition of scale of 2

steps.text: Scale of 2 means each symbol represents 2 items. The KEY tells you the scale. Always check the key first.

elements.description: Horizontal scaled picture graph. Key highlighted showing scale of 2.
Data table visible alongside graph.
Horizontal picture graph with key highlighted. Multiple choice prompt active.
Data table visible.

params.description: Vertical picture graph appears. Favorite Playground Equipment with 4 categories. Key highlighted: Each star = 2 votes. Swings=4 symbols, Slide=3 symbols, Monkey Bars=5 symbols, Sandbox=2 symbols.

steps.text: New graph. First step: check the key. What is the scale of this graph?
What is the scale of this graph?

validator.description: Student correctly identified scale of 2

steps.text: Scale of 2. Each symbol equals 2 votes. Now you can read the graph.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph. Key showing scale of 2.
Vertical picture graph with key highlighted. Multiple choice active.

steps.text: Monkey Bars has 5 symbols. Each symbol equals 2. So how many voted for Monkey Bars?
How many students chose Monkey Bars?

validator.description: Student correctly calculated 5 symbols times scale of 2 equals 10

steps.text: 10 students. 5 symbols, scale of 2. Count by 2s: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph. Monkey Bars highlighted showing 5 symbols.
Vertical picture graph. Monkey Bars category highlighted. Multiple choice active.

params.description: Vertical data table appears showing Favorite Snacks data. Popcorn=8, Pretzels=10, Fruit=12, Crackers=14, Chips=10.
Empty vertical picture graph template appears with scale of 2. Pretzels=5 symbols, Fruit=6 symbols, Crackers=7 symbols already filled. Popcorn and Chips columns empty.

steps.text: Now you create. The table shows 8 students chose Popcorn.
8 is an EVEN number. It groups perfectly into 2s. 8 splits into 4 groups of 2. Place 4 symbols for Popcorn.
Place 4 symbols for Popcorn.

validator.description: Student correctly placed 4 symbols for Popcorn (8 votes)

steps.text: 4 symbols. Each shows 2 votes. 4 groups of 2 is 8.

elements.description: Data table showing Favorite Snacks values.
Vertical picture graph. Popcorn now shows 4 symbols (student-placed). Pretzels, Fruit, Crackers pre-filled. Chips still empty.
Data table visible showing Favorite Snacks data.
Vertical picture graph in creating mode. Popcorn column ready for symbol placement. Pretzels, Fruit, Crackers pre-filled.

steps.text: Good work on Popcorn. Now try Chips. The table shows 10 students chose Chips.
10 is also an EVEN number. It groups into 2s perfectly. Place symbols for Chips.
Place symbols for Chips.

validator.description: Student correctly placed 5 symbols for Chips (10 votes)

steps.text: 5 symbols for 10 votes. You're getting this. Group by 2s, and even numbers give us whole symbols.

elements.description: Data table showing Favorite Snacks values.
Vertical picture graph now complete. All 5 categories filled with symbols.
Data table visible.
Vertical picture graph. Popcorn filled with 4 symbols. Pretzels, Fruit, Crackers pre-filled. Chips column ready for placement.

steps.text: You made a scaled graph. Let's check: which snack got the most votes?
Click on the category with the most votes.

validator.description: Student clicked Crackers (7 symbols, 14 votes)

steps.text: Crackers has the most symbols.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph. Crackers category highlighted showing 7 symbols.
Vertical picture graph in reading mode. Completed Favorite Snacks graph. Key visible. Data table hidden.

steps.text: How many votes did Crackers get?
How many votes did Crackers get?

validator.description: Student correctly calculated 7 symbols times 2 equals 14 votes

steps.text: 14 votes. You counted 7 symbols, and each symbol equals 2. 7 groups of 2 is 14.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph. Crackers highlighted showing 7 symbols.
Vertical picture graph. Crackers highlighted. Multiple choice active.

steps.text: How many MORE students chose Crackers than Popcorn?
How many MORE students chose Crackers than Popcorn?

validator.description: Student correctly calculated 14 minus 8 equals 6

steps.text: 6 more students. Crackers has 14, Popcorn has 8. The difference is 6.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph. Crackers and Popcorn highlighted for comparison.
Vertical picture graph. Crackers and Popcorn categories highlighted. Multiple choice active.

steps.text: You made graphs with numbers like 8, 10, 12, 14. Those are EVEN numbers. They group into 2s perfectly. But what happens with ODD numbers?

params.description: Horizontal data table appears showing Animals We Saw data. Birds=7, Squirrels=10, Rabbits=8, Deer=6.

steps.text: The Key shows Each star equals 2 so we need to group into 2s to make the graph. Look at Birds: 7 sightings. Let's try to group 7 into 2s.

params.description: Animation begins showing 7 items attempting to group into 2s. Groups form: 2, 2, 2... 1 left over.

steps.text: 7 items... 3 groups of 2... and 1 left over.
We have 6 put into groups of 2, but 1 more. What do we do with that 1?

params.description: Animation completes showing 3 full groups and 1 item remaining ungrouped.

steps.text: 7 makes 3 full groups of 2, with 1 left over. How do we show 1 with our symbols?

validator.description: Student identified half-symbol as solution for odd remainder

steps.text: A half-symbol shows 1 item. Let's see how that works.

elements.description: Horizontal data table showing Animals We Saw data. Grouping animation complete showing 3 groups plus 1 leftover.
Horizontal data table with grouping animation showing the problem with 7. Multiple choice active.

params.description: Horizontal picture graph appears showing half-symbol solution. Birds row shows 3 full symbols plus 1 half-symbol.
Animation begins. Key highlighted showing Each star = 2. 3 full symbols appear for 6 items.
Half-symbol appears for the remaining 1 item. Animation completes showing 3 full plus 1 half symbol equals 7.

steps.text: Half of 2 is 1. This half-symbol shows 1 item.
How many sightings does 3 and a half symbols show?
How many sightings does 3 and a half symbols show?

validator.description: Student correctly calculated 3 full symbols plus half-symbol equals 7

steps.text: 7 sightings. 3 full symbols show 6, plus the half-symbol shows 1 more. 6 plus 1 is 7.

elements.description: Horizontal picture graph. Birds row showing 3.5 symbols representing 7 sightings.
Horizontal picture graph showing Birds with 3.5 symbols. Half-symbol demonstration complete. Multiple choice active.

params.description: Horizontal data table appears in symbol display mode showing Animals We Saw data with grouped symbols.
Horizontal picture graph template appears. Squirrels=5 symbols and Deer=3 symbols pre-filled. Birds and Rabbits rows empty.

steps.text: Now you place the symbols to show 7 Bird sightings. Place symbols for Birds. Remember: 7 needs 3 full symbols and 1 half-symbol.
Place symbols for Birds.

validator.description: Student correctly placed 3 full symbols plus 1 half-symbol for Birds (7 sightings)

steps.text: 3 and a half symbols for 7. Each full symbol is 2 sightings, the half is 1 sighting. 6 plus 1 is 7.

elements.description: Horizontal data table in symbol display mode.
Horizontal picture graph. Birds now shows 3.5 symbols (student-placed). Squirrels and Deer pre-filled. Rabbits still empty.
Horizontal data table in symbol display mode showing Animals We Saw data.
Horizontal picture graph in creating mode. Birds row ready for symbol placement. Squirrels and Deer pre-filled.

steps.text: Rabbits have 8 sightings. Does 8 need a half-symbol? Fill in the picture graph for rabbits.
Place symbols for Rabbits.

validator.description: Student correctly placed 4 full symbols for Rabbits (8 sightings, no half-symbol)

steps.text: 4 symbols for 8. 8 is even. It groups perfectly into 2s. No half-symbol needed.

elements.description: Horizontal data table in symbol display mode.
Horizontal picture graph now complete. All 4 categories filled. Birds=3.5 symbols, Squirrels=5 symbols, Rabbits=4 symbols, Deer=3 symbols.
Horizontal data table in symbol display mode.
Horizontal picture graph. Birds=3.5 symbols (student-placed), Squirrels=5 symbols (pre-filled), Rabbits row ready for placement, Deer=3 symbols (pre-filled).

steps.text: How many animals sighted were Birds and Deer IN ALL?
How many animals sighted were Birds and Deer IN ALL?

validator.description: Student correctly calculated 7 plus 6 equals 13

steps.text: 13 birds and deer in all. 7 Birds were sighted, 6 Deer were sighted. 7 plus 6 is 13.

elements.description: Horizontal picture graph in reading mode. Birds and Deer highlighted for combination.
Horizontal picture graph in reading mode. Birds and Deer highlighted. Key visible. Data table hidden. Multiple choice active.

steps.text: How many FEWER Birds were sighted than Squirrels?
How many FEWER Birds were sighted than Squirrels?

validator.description: Student correctly calculated 10 minus 7 equals 3

steps.text: 3 fewer. 10 Squirrels were sighted, 7 Birds were sighted. The difference is 3.

elements.description: Horizontal picture graph. Birds and Squirrels highlighted for comparison.
Horizontal picture graph. Birds (3.5 symbols) and Squirrels (5 symbols) highlighted. Multiple choice active.

params.description: Horizontal data table appears in numeric display mode showing Books Read This Week data. Maya=9, James=12, Sofia=7, Aiden=11.

steps.text: You created scaled picture graphs with EVEN numbers. Those grouped perfectly into 2s. You added HALF-SYMBOLS for ODD numbers because there's always 1 left over. Now let's see if you can handle BOTH together, a mix of even and odd, all on your own.

elements.description: Horizontal data table in numeric display mode showing Books Read This Week data.

params.description: Vertical picture graph template appears with scale of 2. All columns empty. Key visible: Each star = 2 books.

steps.text: Build Maya's row first. Notice the key. Each star equals 2 books. She read 9 books. Think: is 9 even or odd? Will you need a half-symbol?
Place symbols for Maya's 9 books read.

validator.description: Student correctly placed 4.5 symbols for Maya (9 books)

steps.text: 4 and a half symbols. 9 is odd. 8 needs 4 full symbols, plus that leftover 1 needs a half-symbol.

elements.description: Horizontal data table showing Books Read This Week data.
Vertical picture graph. Maya now shows 4.5 symbols (student-placed). James, Sofia, Aiden still empty.
Horizontal data table showing Books Read This Week data.
Vertical picture graph in creating mode. Maya column ready for symbol placement. All columns empty.

steps.text: Build the whole picture graph. Use the key and the table to figure out how many symbols each person needs. Place symbols for all remaining categories.
Place symbols for all remaining categories.

validator.description: Student correctly filled all remaining columns: James=6 symbols, Sofia=3.5 symbols, Aiden=5.5 symbols

steps.text: You built the whole graph. You figured out which numbers needed half-symbols.

elements.description: Horizontal data table showing Books Read This Week data.
Vertical picture graph now complete. Maya=4.5 symbols, James=6 symbols, Sofia=3.5 symbols, Aiden=5.5 symbols.
Horizontal data table visible.
Vertical picture graph. Maya=4.5 symbols (student-placed). James, Sofia, Aiden columns ready for placement.

params.description: System-generated vertical picture graph appears on RIGHT showing same Books Read data at scale of 1. Maya=9 symbols, James=12 symbols, Sofia=7 symbols, Aiden=11 symbols. Key: Each star = 1.

steps.text: Here's the same data, but with scale of 1. Every book gets its OWN symbol.
Look at James. Your graph shows 6 symbols. The scale-of-1 graph shows 12 symbols. Same number of books.
Which graph uses fewer symbols to show the same data?

validator.description: Student selected the scale-of-2 graph as using fewer symbols

steps.text: Scale of 2. Just 6 symbols instead of 12 for James. Same data, fewer symbols. That's why we use scales.
Now let's use YOUR graph to answer some questions.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph at scale of 2, now centered. Scale-of-1 comparison graph removed.
Student's completed vertical picture graph at scale of 2, positioned on left. Key visible.
System-generated vertical picture graph at scale of 1, positioned on right. Same data. Key visible.

steps.text: Use your graph to answer: Who read the most books?
Click on the category with the most books.

validator.description: Student clicked James (12 books, 6 symbols)

steps.text: James read the most. 12 books. 6 symbols, each worth 2.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph. James column highlighted showing 6 symbols.
Vertical picture graph in reading mode. All columns visible. Click category tool active.

steps.text: How many books did James and Aiden read IN ALL?
How many books did James and Aiden read IN ALL?

validator.description: Student correctly calculated 12 plus 11 equals 23

steps.text: 23 books in all. James read 12, Aiden read 11. 12 plus 11 is 23.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph. James and Aiden highlighted for combination.
Vertical picture graph. James (6 symbols) and Aiden (5.5 symbols) highlighted. Multiple choice active.

steps.text: Here's a two-step problem. How many MORE books did Maya and Sofia read together than James?
Step 1: How many books did Maya and Sofia read together?
How many books did Maya and Sofia read in all?

validator.description: Student correctly calculated 9 plus 7 equals 16

steps.text: 16 books together.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph. Maya, Sofia, and James highlighted. Step 1 result displayed: Maya + Sofia = 16.
Vertical picture graph. No categories highlighted yet. Multiple choice active for Step 1.

steps.text: Step 2: Compare James to that total.
James read 12. Maya and Sofia read 16. Who read more, and by how many?

validator.description: Student correctly identified Maya and Sofia read 4 more books (16 minus 12)

steps.text: Maya and Sofia read 4 more books together than James. 16 minus 12 is 4.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph. Maya, Sofia, and James highlighted with step results displayed.
Vertical picture graph. Maya, Sofia, and James highlighted. Step 1 result visible: 16. Multiple choice active for Step 2.

steps.text: You made scaled picture graphs. You know each symbol equals 2. You know half-symbols show 1. Let's see what you know.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph remains briefly visible.
